groups.canonical: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System whitepaper
aliases: Bitcoin whitepaper "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System"
groups.rationale: Unique reference to a specific document title; no strict paraphrase match in the list.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin created in 2008 by Satoshi Nakamoto
groups.rationale: Distinct historical claim about Bitcoin's origin and creator; not a strict paraphrase of any other item.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin as decentralized digital currency without central banks or intermediaries
groups.rationale: Distinct characterization claim about Bitcoin's nature; no same-category paraphrase present.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin blockchain throughput around 7 transactions per second
groups.rationale: Distinct metric about Bitcoin throughput; not equivalent to any other metric.

groups.canonical: Visa throughput around 24,000 transactions per second
groups.rationale: Distinct metric about Visa throughput; no strict paraphrase counterpart.

groups.canonical: Hash-linked blocks forming an immutable chain
groups.rationale: Distinct definition of blockchain structure; not a strict paraphrase of another definition.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin block subsidy started at 50 BTC and halves about every four years
groups.rationale: Distinct quantitative issuance metric; no equivalent metric in the list.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin Proof of Work consensus mechanism
groups.rationale: Distinct method naming Bitcoin's consensus mechanism; not the same claim as mining-process descriptions.

groups.canonical: Miners compete to solve cryptographic puzzles
groups.rationale: Distinct method describing miner behavior; related to Proof of Work but not a strict paraphrase.

groups.canonical: Block reward for adding the next block
groups.rationale: Distinct claim about block addition and rewards; not equivalent to subsidy schedule or mining method entries.

groups.canonical: Ethereum proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015
groups.rationale: Distinct historical claim about Ethereum's proposal and launch dates; no strict paraphrase match.

groups.canonical: Smart contracts as self-executing programs on the blockchain that enforce agreements when conditions are met
groups.rationale: Distinct definition of smart contracts; no equivalent definition present.

groups.canonical: Ethereum energy consumption reduced by ~99.95% after The Merge
aliases: Ethereum energy consumption reduced by ~99.95% after The Merge (Ethereum Foundation claim)
groups.rationale: Distinct result about post-Merge energy reduction; not the same as the Merge event itself.

groups.canonical: Ethereum The Merge transition to Proof of Stake in September 2022
aliases: Ethereum 'The Merge' transition to Proof of Stake (September 2022)
groups.rationale: Distinct claim about a dated protocol transition; related to but not the same as its energy-consumption result.

groups.canonical: Total value locked in DeFi protocols peak
aliases: Total value locked (TVL) in DeFi protocols peak
groups.rationale: Distinct metric about DeFi TVL peak; not a paraphrase of the later decline result.

groups.canonical: Decentralized finance (DeFi) applications
groups.rationale: Distinct definition of DeFi applications; no strict paraphrase equivalent.

groups.canonical: Post-2021 decline in DeFi total value locked
groups.rationale: Distinct result describing a decline in TVL; not equivalent to the TVL peak metric.

groups.canonical: Uniswap pioneered the automated market maker (AMM) model
groups.rationale: Distinct historical claim about Uniswap's role in AMMs; no strict paraphrase present.

groups.canonical: Uniswap trading volume over $1.8 trillion since launch
groups.rationale: Distinct cumulative volume metric; not equivalent to launch year or AMM mechanism items.

groups.canonical: Uniswap launch year (2018)
groups.rationale: Distinct historical claim about Uniswap's launch timing; no strict paraphrase match.

groups.canonical: AMM liquidity pools funded by liquidity providers depositing token pairs
groups.rationale: Distinct definition of the AMM liquidity-pool mechanism; not the same as claims about Uniswap history or volume.

groups.canonical: SEC v. Ripple (2023) ruling on programmatic token sales not necessarily being securities transactions
groups.rationale: Distinct legal result tied to a specific case; not equivalent to broader SEC classification claims.

groups.canonical: SEC classification of most cryptocurrencies as securities with exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum
groups.rationale: Distinct regulatory claim about SEC classification; related to but not a strict paraphrase of the Ripple ruling.
